entry.title: How can SAP EWM improve yard visibility without creating extra bins or unnecessary warehouse tasks?
entry.content:
In many warehouse operations, when trucks arrive in the yard, teams often create additional storage types, yard bins, or extra warehouse tasks just to improve visibility.

However, SAP EWM already provides core visibility through:

✔️ Transportation Unit (TU) tracking
✔️ Delivery linkage
✔️ Material-level information
✔️ Door and checkpoint monitoring

This raises an important operational question:

Can yard-level delivery and TU visibility be leveraged more effectively for planning and execution without adding unnecessary process complexity?

Goal:

- Reduce excess warehouse tasks
- Avoid over-engineered yard structures
- Improve operational clarity
- Simplify yard-to-warehouse flow
- Use standard SAP EWM capabilities more strategically

Discussion Point:

Would a visibility-first approach at yard level improve efficiency better than expanding warehouse structures for control?

I’d value insights from SAP EWM professionals, architects, and project experts on balancing visibility with process simplicity.

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with SAP Cloud Connector: Concept, Setup, and Connecting BTP to Your Local Machi
entry.content: Tip: in a productive scenario, you should not start the SCC from a DOS prompt via go.bat -- you should install and run it as a Windows Service (background process).

entry.title: Functional Programming in ABAP – Part II: Design Strategies and Practical Patterns
entry.content:
In the first part of this series, we explored the fundamental concepts of functional programming and how they can be interpreted in the ABAP world. In this second part, we take a step further, from theory to practice, focusing on how functional programming principles can influence design strategies and patterns in Object-Oriented ABAP. The goal is simple: write code that is not only correct, but also cleaner, more expressive, and easier to evolve. In this section, we focus on design strategies and patterns that align with a functional programming style.

What defines functional programming?

In the first part, we briefly introduced functional programming. In this section, we take a deeper look at the key characteristics that define whether a programming language can be considered a functional programming paradigm. Let’s look at a few.

The theoretical underpinnings

The strictest possible definition would be based on the theoretical foundation of the language. In short, if a language uses lambda calculus as its theoretical framework, it’s a functional language.

First-class functions

A widely accepted definition of a functional programming language supports first-class functions. In practice, this means functions can be treated like any other value: they can be passed as arguments, returned from other functions, and stored in variables.

Immutability

Immutability is one of the most commonly cited characteristics of functional programming. In simple terms, it means that data does not change after it is created. For example, instead of modifying an existing structure or internal table, we create a new structure or internal table with the desired changes. The original data remains unchanged. In functional programming, values are bound to names, and once assigned, they do not change. Rather than updating a value, we create a new one. In the new ABAP syntax, it can be achieved using the following technique

FINAL(greeting) = `Hello, World!`.
Functional Purity

Functional purity refers to the property of functions, which, to be pure, must not have side effects. That is, functions are functions in the strict mathematical sense: they map inputs to outputs, but nothing else — they don’t mutate memory, they don’t perform I/O, etc.

Referential Transparency

Referential transparency is a natural result of pure functions and immutability. In simple terms, it means that a value can always be replaced with its result without changing the program's behavior. For example, if a function always produces the same output for the same input, you can safely replace the function call with its result. This makes the code easier to read and reason about, because you don’t need to track every possible execution path to understand what a value represents.

Higher-Order Functions

Higher-order functions are functions that accept other functions as arguments. This is a natural consequence of first-class functions. In the context of SAP ABAP, I have published a post explaining higher-order functions using an object-oriented approach.

Parametric polymorphism

Parametric polymorphism allows us to use so-called type variables in the type signature. At the use site, type variables are instantiated to concrete types. The implementation must be the same regardless of the specific instantiation. This last part makes it distinct from ad-hoc polymorphism, a.k.a. function overloading, which has unique implementations for each specific instantiation, and thus isn’t universal, i.e., doesn’t allow calling the overloaded function with any type, only the types the function has overloads for.

METHOD handle_parallel IMPORTING param1 TYPE ANY
                                 param2 TYPE REF TO DATA
                       RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO DATA.
Algebraic datatypes

Algebraic datatypes usually refer to the language support for product types (i.e. tuples and records), which most languages have, and sum types (a.k.a. data-carrying enums, a.k.a. discriminated unions), which are somewhat rare. They’re called algebraic because they correspond to structures in abstract algebra, but that’s mostly irrelevant to the discussion at hand — “a rose by any other name” and all that.

ADTs are indeed a prominent feature of languages like Haskell and ML. However, ultimately, it has little bearing on whether a language can be considered functional or not – it’s easy enough to imagine an imperative language with ADTs or a functional one without them. Nice to have, though.

ADTs usually come in a package with pattern matching.

Pattern matching

Pattern matching is also called “destructuring assignment” in some contexts — usually in languages that don’t have proper sum types, and thus pattern matching essentially boils down to syntactic sugar for extracting fields out of records.

There’s also a kind of pattern matching in OOP languages like Java and C# nowadays. However, there it’s closely intertwined with classes and inheritance, rather than ADTs, so similar, but different, and confusingly called the same.

Usually, in its basic form, pattern matching (at least the one we’re interested in here) comes as a case, match, &c, expression, which allows matching on specific variants of a sum type.

Is a bit of a necessity with ADTs, as an eliminator for sum types (i.e., how else would one get the value out of, say, an Option?).

result = SWITCH #( num
                  WHEN 0 THEN 1
                  WHEN 1 THEN 1
                  ELSE num * go_fact( num - 1 ) ).
Recursion

As a reminder, recursion refers to the ability of a function to call itself (directly or indirectly).

It is more or less mandatory, as functional languages generally don’t have structured constructs like loops. This directly follows from immutability and purity: indeed, updating a loop counter is a side effect. The implication is that the only way to achieve looping is recursion.

This has an important consequence, namely, a functional language has to support tail call elimination and/or guarded recursion, so as not to blow out the stack in long loops.

Expression-oriented syntax

Basically, everything is either a declaration or an expression; there are no “statements” in the traditional imperative sense, per se.

This is mostly a feature of using lambda calculus as a guiding principle, if not outright as a formal foundation of the language. Again, more of a consequence than a prerequisite, really, but it has some useful consequences, like the lack of the need for the dreaded ternary operator.

DATA(result) = REDUCE type(
  INIT var = value
  FOR ... [THEN ...] [UNTIL ...]
  NEXT var = calculation
).

DATA(lt_target) = VALUE type( ( ... ) ( ... ) ).
" With FOR
DATA(lt_target) = VALUE type( FOR ... IN ... ( ... ) ).

Design Strategy & Design Pattern

A practical way to apply functional programming principles in SAP ABAP is to clearly separate pure logic from impure operations.

Design Strategy: Separating Pure and Impure Functions

At the core of this approach is a simple idea: not all functions are equal. Some functions perform calculations and do not have any side effects, like mathematical functions, while others interact with external systems, databases, or I/O. Mixing these responsibilities often leads to code that is difficult to understand, test, and maintain.

In this design strategy, we divide functions into two categories:

  • Pure functions
    These behave like mathematical functions. Given the same input, they always produce the same output and do not cause any side effects. They do not modify global state, perform database operations, or call external services. Their responsibility is purely to transform data.
  • Impure functions 
    These functions interact with the outside world (outside the application layer at least). This includes database access, API calls, file operations, or any form of persistence. Because they depend on the external state, their behavior may vary, and they may produce side effects.

By separating these two concerns, we gain several advantages. Pure functions become easy to test, reason about, and reuse. Impure functions, on the other hand, are isolated and controlled, making the overall system more predictable.

Design Pattern: Repository + Data Service

To implement this strategy in ABAP, we can adopt a design pattern inspired by the Repository pattern, combined with a clear separation of responsibilities.

  • The Repository class represents the impure function.
    It is responsible for all interactions with external systems, such as reading from or writing to the database, calling APIs, or I/O. 
  • The Data Service class represents the pure function.
    It contains the business logic and data transformations. It receives data as input, processes it, and returns the result—without performing any side effects.

Hands-on Example: Applying The Design Pattern

Let's walk through a simple example.

Use case: Extract Customer Master Data
  • Retrieve customer master data based on user input, supporting both full load and delta load scenarios.
  • Based on the selected execution mode, the system can:
    • Display the data in ALV
    • Download the data to a local file
    • Download the data to the application server (AL11)
Step 1: Define the interfaces

Design principle: Code to Abstraction, Not Implementation.

In practice, this means we depend on interfaces, not concrete classes. Instead of tightly coupling our logic to a specific implementation, we define contracts that can be implemented in different ways. This principle becomes especially important when separating pure and impure components:

  • The impure function (repository) can have multiple implementations
    (e.g., database, API, mock for testing)
  • The pure function (data service) should not depend on how the data is retrieved and can have multiple implementations.

This is the implementation of Inclusion Polymorphism.

Define the repository interface. This interface abstracts data access and is responsible for retrieving data from the database, either through Open SQL or BAPI calls.

INTERFACE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo
  PUBLIC .
  TYPES: gty_t_company_code_range       TYPE RANGE OF bukrs,
         gty_t_customer_number_range    TYPE RANGE OF kunnr,
         gty_t_credit_ctrl_area_range   TYPE RANGE OF kkber,
         gty_t_credit_acct_range        TYPE RANGE OF knkli,
         gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE RANGE OF ktokd,
         gty_t_change_date_range        TYPE RANGE OF cddatum.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_crdt_master,
           syst_id          TYPE syst_sysid,
           company_code     TYPE bukrs,
           customer_number  TYPE kunnr,
           customer_name    TYPE text100,
           credit_ctrl_area TYPE kkber,
           credit_acct      TYPE knkli,
           credit_acct_name TYPE text100,
           risk_categ       TYPE ctlpc_cm,
           term             TYPE dzterm,
           credit_limit     TYPE klimk,
           currency         TYPE waers,
           credit_blocked   TYPE crblb_cm,
           status_cust      TYPE char10,
         END OF gty_s_crdt_master,

         gty_t_crdt_master TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF gty_s_crdt_master WITH EMPTY KEY.

  METHODS find_all IMPORTING it_company_code_range       TYPE gty_t_company_code_range
                             it_customer_number_range    TYPE gty_t_customer_number_range
                             it_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                             it_credit_ctrl_area_range   TYPE gty_t_credit_ctrl_area_range
                             it_credit_acct_range        TYPE gty_t_credit_acct_range
                             iv_credit_blocked           TYPE crblb_cm
                   RETURNING VALUE(result)               TYPE gty_t_crdt_master.

  METHODS find_delta IMPORTING it_company_code_range       TYPE gty_t_company_code_range
                               it_customer_number_range    TYPE gty_t_customer_number_range
                               it_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                               it_credit_ctrl_area_range   TYPE gty_t_credit_ctrl_area_range
                               it_credit_acct_range        TYPE gty_t_credit_acct_range
                               it_change_date_range        TYPE gty_t_change_date_range
                               iv_credit_blocked           TYPE crblb_cm
                     RETURNING VALUE(result)               TYPE gty_t_crdt_master.

  METHODS find_new_cust IMPORTING it_company_code_range       TYPE gty_t_company_code_range
                                  it_customer_number_range    TYPE gty_t_customer_number_range
                                  it_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                        RETURNING VALUE(result)               TYPE gty_t_crdt_master.

ENDINTERFACE.

Next, define two data service interfaces. These interfaces are responsible for handling data transformation and mapping.

INTERFACE zif_zgxxs_parser
  PUBLIC .

  METHODS parse IMPORTING iv_separator TYPE char1
                          iv_delimiter TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                          it_source_data TYPE ANY TABLE
                          iv_include_header TYPE abap_bool DEFAULT abap_true
                RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE stringtab.

  METHODS parse_binary IMPORTING iv_separator TYPE char1
                          iv_delimiter TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                          it_source_data TYPE ANY TABLE
                          iv_include_header TYPE abap_bool DEFAULT abap_true
                RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE xstringtab.

ENDINTERFACE.

zif_zgxxs_parser defines an abstraction for parsing data from internal table format into a text-based file format.

INTERFACE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser
  PUBLIC .

  TYPES: BEGIN OF lty_s_objectid,
           objectid TYPE cdobjectv,
         END OF lty_s_objectid,

         lty_t_objectid TYPE HASHED TABLE OF lty_s_objectid WITH UNIQUE KEY primary_key COMPONENTS objectid.

  METHODS get_updated_customer IMPORTING it_changed_docs TYPE cdredcd_tab
                               RETURNING VALUE(result)   TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_customer_number_range.

  METHODS get_key_tabs IMPORTING it_changed_docs TYPE cdredcd_tab
                       RETURNING VALUE(result)   TYPE REF TO data.

ENDINTERFACE.

zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser This interface abstracts the pure function responsible for mapping data.

Step 2: Write the Class Implementation

We now move on to the class implementation.

CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

    TYPES: lty_t_udate_range TYPE RANGE OF cddatum,
           lty_t_objectid_range TYPE RANGE OF cdobjectv,
           lty_t_tabname_range TYPE RANGE OF tabname,
           lty_t_fname_range TYPE RANGE OF fieldname.

    TYPES: lty_t_crdt_master TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_s_crdt_master WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS status_cust.

    CONSTANTS: BEGIN OF lc_tvarv_name,
                objclass TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_OBJCLASS',
                tabname TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_TABNAME',
                fname TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_FNAME',
                tcode TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_TCODE',
               END OF lc_tvarv_name.

    DATA go_service TYPE REF TO zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser.

    METHODS get_updated_customers IMPORTING it_udate_range    TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_change_date_range
                                            it_objectid_range TYPE lty_t_objectid_range
                                  RETURNING VALUE(result)     TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_customer_number_range.

    METHODS get_changed_documents IMPORTING it_udate_range TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_change_date_range
                                            it_objectid    TYPE cdobjectv_range_tab
                                  RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE cdredcd_tab.

    METHODS get_variant_variables IMPORTING iv_name TYPE rvari_vnam
                                  RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE tvarvc_t.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MASTER_REPO IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_changed_documents.
    DATA lt_cdred TYPE cdredcd_tab.
    DATA ls_result LIKE LINE OF result.
    DATA lt_objectid TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser=>lty_t_objectid.


    DATA(lt_objclass_range) = VALUE cdobjectcl_range_tab( FOR ls_obj IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-objclass )
                                                          ( sign = ls_obj-sign option = ls_obj-opti
                                                            low = ls_obj-low high = ls_obj-high ) ).


    DATA(lt_tabname_range) = VALUE lty_t_tabname_range( FOR ls_tabname IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-tabname )
                                                          ( sign = ls_tabname-sign option = ls_tabname-opti
                                                            low = ls_tabname-low high = ls_tabname-high ) ).


    DATA(lt_fname_range) = VALUE lty_t_fname_range( FOR ls_fname IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-fname )
                                                    ( sign = ls_fname-sign option = ls_fname-opti
                                                      low = ls_fname-low high = ls_fname-high ) ).

    DATA(lt_tcode_range) = VALUE cdtcode_range_tab( FOR ls_tcode IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-tcode )
                                                    ( sign = ls_tcode-sign option = ls_tcode-opti
                                                      low = ls_tcode-low high = ls_tcode-high ) ).

    ASSIGN it_udate_range[ 1 ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_udate>).
    IF <fs_l_udate> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    DATA(lv_date_until) = COND cddatum( WHEN <fs_l_udate>-high IS INITIAL
                                        THEN <fs_l_udate>-low
                                        ELSE <fs_l_udate>-high ).

    CALL FUNCTION 'CHANGEDOCUMENT_READ_ALL'
      EXPORTING
        i_date_of_change           = <fs_l_udate>-low    " From-change date for search
*       i_time_of_change           = '000000'    " From-change time for search
        i_date_until               = lv_date_until    " Change date up to which you want to search
*       i_time_until               = '235959'    " Latest change time in search
        it_objectclass             = lt_objclass_range    " Range Table Type for CDOBJECTCL for Function Module Interface
        it_objectid                = it_objectid
        it_tcode                   = lt_tcode_range    " Range table type for TCODE for function module interface
        it_tablename               = lt_tabname_range   " Range Table Type for CDTABNAME f. Function Module Interface
      IMPORTING
        et_cdred                   = lt_cdred    " Formatted change documents with header data
      EXCEPTIONS
        missing_input_objectclass  = 1
        missing_input_header       = 2
        no_position_found          = 3
        wrong_access_to_archive    = 4
        time_zone_conversion_error = 5
        read_too_many_entries      = 6
        OTHERS                     = 7.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*     MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
*                WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    result = VALUE #( FOR line IN lt_cdred
                      WHERE ( fname IN lt_fname_range )
                     ( line ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_updated_customers.
  ...
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_variant_variables.

    SELECT *
      FROM tvarvc
      WHERE name eq _name
    INTO TABLE @result.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_all.
    DATA result_tmp TYPE lty_t_crdt_master.

    " include new customer
    IF it_credit_ctrl_area_range IS INITIAL
      AND it_credit_acct_range IS INITIAL.

      SELECT -sysid AS syst_id
             , b~bukrs AS company_code
             , k~kunnr AS customer_number
             , a~name1 &&  && a~name2 AS customer_name
             , k~kkber AS credit_ctrl_area
             , k~knkli AS credit_acct
             , CASE k~knkli
                WHEN ' ' THEN 
                ELSE n~name1 &&  && n~name2
               END AS credit_acct_name
             , k~ctlpc AS risk_categ
             , b~zterm AS term
             , k~klimk AS credit_limit
            " Begin of changes RH0001
             , t~waers AS currency
             " End of changes RH0001
             , k~crblb AS credit_blocked
             , CASE WHEN a~sperr EQ  AND a~loevm EQ 
                    THEN 'ACTIVE'
                    ELSE 'INACTIVE'
               END AS status_cust
      FROM knb1 AS b
        INNER JOIN t001cm AS c ON b~bukrs = c~bukrs
        INNER JOIN kna1 AS a ON b~kunnr = a~kunnr
        INNER JOIN knkk AS k ON a~kunnr = k~kunnr
                            AND c~kkber = k~kkber
        LEFT OUTER JOIN t014 AS t ON k~kkber = t~kkber "#EC CI_BUFFJOIN
        LEFT OUTER JOIN kna1 AS n ON n~kunnr = k~knkli
      WHERE b~bukrs IN _company_code_range
        AND b~kunnr IN _customer_number_range
        AND a~ktokd IN _acct_assignment_grp_cust
        AND k~kkber IN _credit_ctrl_area_range
        AND k~knkli IN _credit_acct_range
      INTO TABLE @result_tmp.

      LOOP AT zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_new_cust(
            it_company_code_range       = it_company_code_range
            it_customer_number_range    = it_customer_number_range
            it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
        ) INTO DATA(ls_new_cust).

        INSERT ls_new_cust INTO TABLE result_tmp.

      ENDLOOP.


    ELSE.

      SELECT -sysid AS syst_id
             , b~bukrs AS company_code
             , k~kunnr AS customer_number
             , a~name1 &&  && a~name2 AS customer_name
             , k~kkber AS credit_ctrl_area
             , k~knkli AS credit_acct
             , CASE k~knkli
                WHEN ' ' THEN 
                ELSE n~name1 &&  && n~name2
               END AS credit_acct_name
             , k~ctlpc AS risk_categ
             , b~zterm AS term
             , k~klimk AS credit_limit
             , t~waers AS currency
             , k~crblb AS credit_blocked
             , CASE WHEN a~sperr EQ  AND a~loevm EQ 
                    THEN 'ACTIVE'
                    ELSE 'INACTIVE'
               END AS status_cust
      FROM knb1 AS b
        INNER JOIN t001cm AS c ON b~bukrs = c~bukrs
        INNER JOIN kna1 AS a ON b~kunnr = a~kunnr
        INNER JOIN knkk AS k ON a~kunnr = k~kunnr
                            AND c~kkber = k~kkber
        LEFT OUTER JOIN t014 AS t ON k~kkber = t~kkber "#EC CI_BUFFJOIN
        LEFT OUTER JOIN kna1 AS n ON n~kunnr = k~knkli
      WHERE b~bukrs IN _company_code_range
        AND b~kunnr IN _customer_number_range
        AND a~ktokd IN _acct_assignment_grp_cust
        AND k~kkber IN _credit_ctrl_area_range
        AND k~knkli IN _credit_acct_range
      INTO TABLE @result_tmp.

    ENDIF.

    result = SWITCH #( iv_active_cust_only
                       WHEN abap_false THEN CORRESPONDING #( result_tmp )
                       ELSE FILTER #( result_tmp USING KEY primary_key
                                      WHERE status_cust = CONV #( 'ACTIVE' ) ) ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_delta.

    DATA lt_result TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_s_crdt_master WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY primary_key
                                                                                      COMPONENTS customer_number company_code credit_ctrl_area.
    DATA lt_result_kna1 TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_result_knb1 TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_result_knkk TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_result_merge TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_s_crdt_master WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY primary_key
                                                                                      COMPONENTS customer_number company_code credit_ctrl_area.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service=>gty_t_keytab.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> TYPE zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_kna1=>gty_t_keytab_kna1.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> TYPE zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_knb1=>gty_t_keytab_knb1.
    go_service = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service( ).
    " get changed documents
    DATA(lt_changed_docs) = get_changed_documents(
                            it_udate_range = it_change_date_range
                            it_objectid    = VALUE #( FOR line IN it_customer_number_range
                                                      ( sign = line-sign option = line-option low = line-low high = line-high ) )
                        ).
    IF lt_changed_docs IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get updated customers
    DATA(lt_updated_customers) = go_service->get_updated_customer( lt_changed_docs ).
    IF lt_updated_customers IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " extract data
    lt_result = CORRESPONDING #( zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_all(
       EXPORTING
         it_company_code_range       = it_company_code_range
         it_customer_number_range    = lt_updated_customers
         it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
         it_credit_ctrl_area_range   = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
         it_credit_acct_range        = it_credit_acct_range
         iv_active_cust_only         = iv_active_cust_only
     ) ).
    IF lt_result IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    DATA(lo_keytab) = go_service->get_key_tabs( lt_changed_docs ).
    ASSIGN lo_keytab->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS ASSIGNED
      AND <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT INITIAL.

      lt_result_knkk = FILTER #( lt_result IN <lfs_t_keytab> USING KEY primary_key
                          WHERE customer_number = kunnr
                            AND credit_ctrl_area = kkber ).

    ENDIF.

    go_service = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_kna1( ).
    DATA(lo_keytab_kna1) = go_service->get_key_tabs( lt_changed_docs ).
    ASSIGN lo_keytab_kna1->* TO <lfs_t_keytab_kna1>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> IS ASSIGNED
      AND <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> IS NOT INITIAL.

      lt_result_kna1 = FILTER #( lt_result IN <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> USING KEY primary_key
                                 WHERE customer_number = kunnr ).

    ENDIF.

    go_service = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_knb1( ).
    DATA(lo_keytab_knb1) = go_service->get_key_tabs( lt_changed_docs ).
    ASSIGN lo_keytab_knb1->* TO <lfs_t_keytab_knb1>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> IS ASSIGNED
      AND <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> IS NOT INITIAL.

      lt_result_knb1 = FILTER #( lt_result IN <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> USING KEY primary_key
                                 WHERE customer_number = kunnr
                                   AND company_code = bukrs ).

    ENDIF.

    lt_result_merge = CORRESPONDING #( lt_result_kna1 ).

    LOOP AT lt_result_knb1 INTO DATA(ls_result_knb1).
      IF NOT line_exists( lt_result_merge[ KEY primary_key
                                           COMPONENTS customer_number = ls_result_knb1-customer_number
                                                      company_code = ls_result_knb1-company_code
                                                      credit_ctrl_area = ls_result_knb1-credit_ctrl_area ] ).

        INSERT ls_result_knb1 INTO TABLE lt_result_merge.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT lt_result_knkk INTO DATA(ls_result_knkk).
      IF NOT line_exists( lt_result_merge[ KEY primary_key
                                           COMPONENTS customer_number = ls_result_knkk-customer_number
                                                      company_code = ls_result_knkk-company_code
                                                      credit_ctrl_area = ls_result_knb1-credit_ctrl_area ] ).

        INSERT ls_result_knkk INTO TABLE lt_result_merge.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

    result = CORRESPONDING #( lt_result_merge ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_new_cust.

    SELECT -sysid AS syst_id
           , b~bukrs AS company_code
           , b~kunnr AS customer_number
           , a~name1 &&  && a~name2 AS customer_name
           , b~zterm AS term
           , CASE WHEN a~sperr EQ  AND a~loevm EQ 
                  THEN 'ACTIVE'
                  ELSE 'INACTIVE'
             END AS status_cust
    FROM knb1 AS b
      INNER JOIN t001cm AS c ON b~bukrs = c~bukrs
      INNER JOIN kna1 AS a ON b~kunnr = a~kunnr
    WHERE b~bukrs IN _company_code_range
      AND b~kunnr IN _customer_number_range
      AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT kunnr
                        FROM knkk AS kn  "#EC CI_BUFFSUBQ
                        WHERE kn~kunnr eq b~kunnr
              )
      AND a~ktokd IN _acct_assignment_grp_cust
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @result.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

The class zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo implements the interface zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo and is responsible for retrieving data from the database. As it performs data access operations, this class represents the impure function, where side effects are introduced. In addition to data retrieval, this class depends on the interfacezif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser, which serves as the abstraction of the data service. 

Next, we move on to the implementation of the parser class.

CLASS zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_zgxxs_parser .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    DATA gt_components TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table.
    METHODS build_header_line IMPORTING iv_separator   TYPE char1
                                        iv_delimiter   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                        is_source_data TYPE any
                              RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE string.

    METHODS build_data_line IMPORTING iv_separator   TYPE char1
                                      iv_delimiter   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                      is_source_data TYPE any
                            RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE string.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_DAT_PARSER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD build_data_line.
    DATA lv_separator TYPE char1.
    DATA lv_delimiter TYPE char1.

    LOOP AT gt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name OF STRUCTURE is_source_data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_data>).
      IF <fs_l_data> IS ASSIGNED.
        result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                 |{ <fs_l_data> }{ lv_delimiter }|.
        lv_separator = iv_separator.
        lv_delimiter = iv_delimiter.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD build_header_line.

    result = REDUCE #( INIT file_data TYPE string
                       sep = ''
                       delimit = ''
                       FOR line IN gt_components
                       NEXT
                       file_data = |{ file_data }{ sep }{ delimit }{ line-name }{ delimit }|
                       sep = iv_separator
                       delimit = iv_delimiter ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_parser~parse.

    IF it_source_data IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get the components
    gt_components = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( CAST cl_abap_tabledescr( cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data( it_source_data )
      )->get_table_line_type( ) )->get_components( ).

    LOOP AT it_source_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_line>).
      IF sy-tabix EQ 1
        AND iv_include_header EQ abap_true.

        INSERT build_header_line(
             iv_separator   = iv_separator
             iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
             is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) INTO TABLE result.

      ENDIF.

      INSERT build_data_line(
           iv_separator   = iv_separator
           iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
           is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) INTO TABLE result.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_parser~parse_binary.

    DATA lv_result TYPE xstring.

    IF it_source_data IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get the components
    gt_components = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( CAST cl_abap_tabledescr( cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data( it_source_data )
      )->get_table_line_type( ) )->get_components( ).

    DATA(lo_converter) = cl_abap_conv_out_ce=>create( encoding = 'UTF-8' ).

    LOOP AT it_source_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_line>).

      IF sy-tabix EQ 1
        AND iv_include_header EQ abap_true.

        CLEAR lv_result.

        lo_converter->convert(
        EXPORTING
          data                          = build_header_line( iv_separator   = iv_separator
                                                             iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
                                                             is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) && cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline " Field to Be Converted
        IMPORTING
          buffer                        = lv_result    " Converted Data
        ).

        INSERT lv_result INTO TABLE result.

      ENDIF.

      CLEAR lv_result.

      lo_converter->convert(
        EXPORTING
          data                          = build_data_line( iv_separator   = iv_separator
                                                           iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
                                                           is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) && cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline " Field to Be Converted
        IMPORTING
          buffer                        = lv_result    " Converted Data
      ).
*        CATCH cx_sy_codepage_converter_init.    "
*        CATCH cx_sy_conversion_codepage.    "
*        CATCH cx_parameter_invalid_type.    "
      INSERT lv_result INTO TABLE result.

    ENDLOOP.


  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser_cred_mast DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    METHODS build_header_line REDEFINITION.
    METHODS build_data_line REDEFINITION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_currency  IMPORTING is_source_data TYPE any
                          RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE waers.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_DAT_PARSER_CRED_MAST IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD build_data_line.
    DATA lv_separator TYPE char1.
    DATA lv_delimiter TYPE char1.
    DATA lv_output TYPE c LENGTH 20.
    DATA lv_source_val TYPE string. " RH0001

    DATA(lv_waers) = get_currency( is_source_data ).

    LOOP AT gt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name OF STRUCTURE is_source_data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_data>).
      IF <fs_l_data> IS ASSIGNED.
        DATA(lt_ddic_obj) = ls_component-type->get_ddic_object( ).
        ASSIGN lt_ddic_obj[ 1 ]-dtyp TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_dtyp>).
        ASSIGN lt_ddic_obj[ 1 ]-tabname TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_tabname>).

        " Begin of change RH0001
        IF <fs_l_dtyp> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <fs_l_dtyp> EQ 'CURR'.
            WRITE <fs_l_data> TO lv_output NO-GROUPING CURRENCY lv_waers.
            CONDENSE lv_output NO-GAPS.
            result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                   |{ lv_output }{ lv_delimiter }|.

            CONTINUE.

          ENDIF.


        ENDIF.

        IF <fs_l_tabname> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <fs_l_tabname> EQ 'KUNNR'
            OR <fs_l_tabname> EQ 'KNKLI'.
            lv_source_val = <fs_l_data>.
            DATA(lv_ext_number) = |{ lv_source_val ALPHA = OUT }|.
            CONDENSE lv_ext_number NO-GAPS.
            result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                   |{ lv_ext_number }{ lv_delimiter }|.
            CONTINUE.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.

        result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                 |{ <fs_l_data> }{ lv_delimiter }|.
        " End of change RH0001

        lv_separator = iv_separator.
        lv_delimiter = iv_delimiter.

        UNASSIGN: <fs_l_dtyp>, <fs_l_tabname>. " RH0001

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD build_header_line.

    DATA(lt_header_map) = VALUE lty_t_header_map(
      ( name = 'SYST_ID' description = 'SAP System' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'COMPANY_CODE' description = 'Company Code' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CUSTOMER_NUMBER' description = 'Customer Number' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CUSTOMER_NAME' description = 'Customer Name' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_CTRL_AREA' description = 'Credit Control Area' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_ACCT' description = 'Credit Account' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_ACCT_NAME' description = 'Credit Account Name' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'RISK_CATEG' description = 'Risk Category' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'TERM' description = 'Terms of Payment' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_LIMIT' description = 'Credit Limit' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CURRENCY' description = 'Currency' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_BLOCKED' description = 'Credit Blocked Remark' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'STATUS_CUST' description = 'Status Customer' ) ##NO_TEXT
       ).

    result = REDUCE #( INIT file_data TYPE string
                       sep = ''
                       delimit = ''
                       FOR line IN gt_components
                       LET desc = VALUE string( lt_header_map[ name = line-name ]-description DEFAULT line-name )
                       IN
                       NEXT
                       file_data = |{ file_data }{ sep }{ delimit }{ desc }{ delimit }|
                       sep = iv_separator
                       delimit = iv_delimiter ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_currency.

    LOOP AT gt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name OF STRUCTURE is_source_data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_data>).
      IF <fs_l_data> IS ASSIGNED.
        DATA(lt_ddic_obj) = ls_component-type->get_ddic_object( ).
        ASSIGN lt_ddic_obj[ 1 ]-dtyp TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_dtyp>).
        IF <fs_l_dtyp> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <fs_l_dtyp> EQ 'CUKY'.
            result = <fs_l_data>.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

There are two implementation classes, organized using a parent–child (inheritance) relationship:

  • zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser
    Acts as the base (parent) class, providing common parsing logic.
  • zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser_cred_mast
    A specialized parser for credit master data, implemented as a child class of the base parser.

To demonstrate that this design is easy to test, we will present a test class for the parser functionality.

*"* use this source file for your ABAP unit test classes
CLASS ltcl_parser DEFINITION FOR TESTING
                  RISK LEVEL HARMLESS
                  DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA cut TYPE REF TO zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser_cred_mast.
    METHODS: setup,
             test_initial FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check,
             test_cust_leading_zero FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check,
             test_crcdt_leading_zero FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_parser IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD setup.
    cut = NEW #( ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_initial.

    " given
    DATA(lt_source) = VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master(
                      ( syst_id = 'DUM'
                        company_code = '1687'
                        customer_number = '10199751'
                        customer_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        credit_ctrl_area = 'DUM01'
                        credit_acct = '10199751'
                        credit_acct_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        risk_categ = 'RC1'
                        term = 'TERM01'
                        credit_limit = 34000
                        currency = 'AUD' ) ).

    " when
    DATA(act) = cut->zif_zgxxs_parser~parse(
                iv_separator      = '|'
                it_source_data    = lt_source ).

    " then
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = act ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_cust_leading_zero.

    " given
    DATA(lt_source) = VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master(
                      ( syst_id = 'DUM'
                        company_code = '1687'
                        customer_number = '0000102723'
                        customer_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        credit_ctrl_area = 'CCT01'
                        credit_acct = '10199751'
                        credit_acct_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        risk_categ = 'RC1'
                        term = 'TERM01'
                        credit_limit = 34000
                        currency = 'AUD' ) ).

    " when
    DATA(act) = cut->zif_zgxxs_parser~parse(
                iv_separator      = '|'
                it_source_data    = lt_source ).

    " then
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals(
      EXPORTING
        act                  = act[ 2 ]    " Data object with current value
        exp                  = `DUM|1687|102723|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|CCT01|10199751|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|RC1|TERM01|34000.00|AUD||`
        msg                  = 'Customer Number without leading zeros'    " Description
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_crcdt_leading_zero.

    " given
    DATA(lt_source) = VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master(
                      ( syst_id = 'DUM'
                        company_code = '1687'
                        customer_number = '0000102723'
                        customer_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        credit_ctrl_area = 'CCT01'
                        credit_acct = '0000100300'
                        credit_acct_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        risk_categ = 'RC1'
                        term = 'TERM02'
                        currency = 'AUD' ) ).

    " when
    DATA(act) = cut->zif_zgxxs_parser~parse(
                iv_separator      = '|'
                it_source_data    = lt_source ).

    " then
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals(
      EXPORTING
        act                  = act[ 2 ]    " Data object with current value
        exp                  = `DUM|1687|102723|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|CCT01|100300|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|RC1|TERM02|0.00|AUD||`
        msg                  = 'Customer Number without leading zeros'    " Description
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Next, we move on to another pure function implementation.

CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_keytab,
            kunnr TYPE kunnr,
            kkber TYPE kkber,
           END OF gty_s_keytab,

           gty_t_keytab TYPE HASHED TABLE OF gty_s_keytab WITH UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS kunnr kkber.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MASTER_SERVICE IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE gty_t_keytab.

    result = NEW gty_t_keytab( ).
    ASSIGN result->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_changed_doc)
      WHERE tabname EQ 'KNKK'.

      IF ls_changed_doc-tabkey IS NOT INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_kunnr) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+3(10).
        DATA(lv_kkber) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+13(4).
        IF NOT line_exists( <lfs_t_keytab>[ KEY primary_key
                                             COMPONENTS kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                                        kkber = lv_kkber ] ).

          INSERT VALUE gty_s_keytab( kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                     kkber = lv_kkber ) INTO TABLE <lfs_t_keytab>.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_updated_customer.
    DATA lt_objectid TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser=>lty_t_objectid.
    DATA ls_result LIKE LINE OF result.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_cdred).

      IF ls_cdred-objectid IS NOT INITIAL
        AND NOT line_exists( lt_objectid[ KEY primary_key
                                          COMPONENTS objectid = ls_cdred-objectid ] ).

        ls_result = VALUE #( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = ls_cdred-objectid ).
        INSERT ls_result INTO TABLE result.
        INSERT VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser=>lty_s_objectid( objectid = ls_cdred-objectid ) INTO TABLE lt_objectid.

        CLEAR ls_result.

      ENDIF.


    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_kna1 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_keytab_kna1,
            kunnr TYPE kunnr,
           END OF gty_s_keytab_kna1,

           gty_t_keytab_kna1 TYPE HASHED TABLE OF gty_s_keytab_kna1 WITH UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS kunnr.

    METHODS zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs REDEFINITION.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MAST_SERV_KNA1 IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE gty_t_keytab_kna1.

    result = NEW gty_t_keytab_kna1( ).
    ASSIGN result->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_changed_doc)
      WHERE tabname EQ 'KNA1'.

      IF ls_changed_doc-tabkey IS NOT INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_kunnr) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+3(10).
        IF NOT line_exists( <lfs_t_keytab>[ KEY primary_key
                                             COMPONENTS kunnr = lv_kunnr ] ).

          INSERT VALUE gty_s_keytab_kna1( kunnr = lv_kunnr ) INTO TABLE <lfs_t_keytab>.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_knb1 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_keytab_knb1,
            kunnr TYPE kunnr,
            bukrs TYPE bukrs,
           END OF gty_s_keytab_knb1,

           gty_t_keytab_knb1 TYPE HASHED TABLE OF gty_s_keytab_knb1 WITH UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS kunnr bukrs.

    METHODS zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs REDEFINITION.

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MAST_SERV_KNB1 IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE gty_t_keytab_knb1.

    result = NEW gty_t_keytab_knb1( ).
    ASSIGN result->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_changed_doc)
      WHERE tabname EQ 'KNB1'.

      IF ls_changed_doc-tabkey IS NOT INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_kunnr) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+3(10).
        DATA(lv_bukrs) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+13(4).
        IF NOT line_exists( <lfs_t_keytab>[ KEY primary_key
                                             COMPONENTS kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                                        bukrs = lv_bukrs ] ).

          INSERT VALUE gty_s_keytab_knb1( kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                     bukrs = lv_bukrs ) INTO TABLE <lfs_t_keytab>.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

We follow the same approach as in the parser implementation by using inheritance.

In this case, we define one base (parent) class and two child classes. The base class contains the common logic, while each child class handles table-specific logic for getting the table keys.

As discussed earlier, the repository class depends on the data service layer, and these implementations are invoked within the repository class. However, with this design, the data service class can be tested independently and deterministically. For repository testing, the data service dependency can be mocked via dependency injection, enabling isolated unit tests without coupling to the actual implementation.

Step 3: Process Orchestration

At this stage, we move on to the main program, where the overall process flow is orchestrated, and the different components are brought together.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  lcl_main=>create_instance( )->run(
    EXPORTING
      iv_run_type   = SWITCH #( p_disp
                                WHEN abap_true THEN lcl_main=>gc_run_type-display
                                ELSE lcl_main=>gc_run_type-interface )
      iv_data_type  = COND #( WHEN rb_delta EQ abap_true
                              THEN lcl_main=>gc_data_type-delta
                              ELSE lcl_main=>gc_data_type-full )
      iv_target_system = SWITCH #( p_local
                                  WHEN abap_true THEN lcl_main=>gc_target_system-local
                                  ELSE lcl_main=>gc_target_system-server )
      it_company_code_range = so_cocd[]
      . . .
  ).
  METHOD run.
    DATA lt_split_credit_master TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_download_return TYPE bapiret2_t.
    DATA ls_down_ret TYPE bapiret2.

    DATA(lv_tcode) = SWITCH tcode( sy-batch
                                        WHEN abap_true THEN gc_transaction
                                        ELSE sy-tcode ).

    CALL FUNCTION 'AUTHORITY_CHECK_TCODE'
      EXPORTING
        tcode  = lv_tcode    " Transaction Code
      EXCEPTIONS
        ok     = 1
        not_ok = 2
        others = 3.
    IF sy-subrc NE 1.
      MESSAGE |User: { sy-uname } not authorized to execute the tcode| TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.


    DATA(ls_validate_ret) = validate_input(
                            iv_run_type                 = iv_run_type
                            iv_data_type                = iv_data_type
                            iv_target_system            = iv_target_system
                            it_company_code_range       = it_company_code_range
                            it_customer_number_range    = it_customer_number_range
                            it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                            it_credit_ctrl_area_range   = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
                            it_credit_acct_range        = it_credit_acct_range
                            it_udate_range              = it_udate_range
                            iv_local_file               = iv_local_file
                            iv_server_path              = iv_server_path
                            iv_blocked                  = iv_blocked
                            iv_max_data                 = iv_max_data
                        ).
    IF ls_validate_ret IS NOT INITIAL.
      MESSAGE ls_validate_ret-message TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE ls_validate_ret-type.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get data
    go_credit_master_repo = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo02( ). 
    DATA(lt_credit_master) = SWITCH zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master( iv_data_type
                                       WHEN gc_data_type-full THEN go_credit_master_repo->find_all_act(
                                                                       it_company_code_range     = it_company_code_range
                                                                       it_customer_number_range  = it_customer_number_range
                                                                       it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                                                                       it_credit_ctrl_area_range = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
                                                                       it_credit_acct_range      = it_credit_acct_range
                                                                       iv_active_cust_only       = iv_active_cust_only )
                                       ELSE go_credit_master_repo->find_delta_act(
                                                                       it_company_code_range     = it_company_code_range
                                                                       it_customer_number_range  = it_customer_number_range
                                                                       it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                                                                       it_credit_ctrl_area_range = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
                                                                       it_credit_acct_range      = it_credit_acct_range
                                                                       it_change_date_range      = it_udate_range
                                                                       iv_active_cust_only       = iv_active_cust_only ) ).

    IF lt_credit_master IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE TEXT-E01
      TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    IF iv_run_type EQ gc_run_type-interface.

      DATA(lv_counter) = 0.
      LOOP AT lt_credit_master INTO DATA(ls_credit_mast).
        lv_counter = lv_counter + 1.
        INSERT ls_credit_mast INTO TABLE lt_split_credit_master.

        IF lv_counter EQ iv_max_data.
          CLEAR lv_counter.

          INSERT download( it_source_data = lt_split_credit_master
                          iv_target_system = iv_target_system
                          iv_local_file  = iv_local_file
                          iv_server_path = iv_server_path ) INTO TABLE lt_download_return.

          CLEAR lt_split_credit_master.

        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.

      IF lt_split_credit_master IS NOT INITIAL.
        INSERT download( it_source_data = lt_split_credit_master
                          iv_target_system = iv_target_system
                          iv_local_file  = iv_local_file
                          iv_server_path = iv_server_path ) INTO TABLE lt_download_return.

      ENDIF.


      IF line_exists( lt_download_return[ type = 'S' ] ).

        WRITE / TEXT-M01
        .

        LOOP AT lt_download_return INTO ls_down_ret.
          WRITE / ls_down_ret-message.

        ENDLOOP.

      ENDIF.

      IF line_exists( lt_download_return[ type = 'E' ] ).

        WRITE / TEXT-M02
        .

        LOOP AT lt_download_return INTO ls_down_ret.
          WRITE / ls_down_ret-message.

        ENDLOOP.

      ENDIF.

    ELSE.
      " display alv
      go_alv = NEW lcl_salv( ).
      go_alv->display_alv( lt_credit_master ).


    ENDIF.


  ENDMETHOD.

Conclusion

This post focuses on the design approach rather than detailed implementation or specific syntax. The goal is to illustrate how functional programming principles can be applied in SAP ABAP at an architectural/ design level.

The sequence of steps presented is not mandatory; it is structured this way to make this blog post easier to follow. In practice, the development approach may vary—developers may choose a top-down or bottom-up approach depending on their preferences.

While every effort has been made to present the concepts clearly, some areas may benefit from further refinement or clarification. Feedback and discussion are always welcome to improve and deepen the understanding of this approach.

References

  1. Serokell — Rust Is Not a Functional Language
    A discussion on what defines functional programming and why certain languages only partially adopt its principles.
    https://serokell.io/blog/rust-is-not-a-functional-language
  2. John Carmack — Carmack on Inlined Code
    Insights on code structure, simplicity, and performance trade-offs in software design.
    http://number-none.com/blow/blog/programming/2014/09/26/carmack-on-inlined-code.html

entry.title: Re: Learning confusion
entry.content:
Dear @Nishant_010

You question is very familiar to me as every day i face bunch of students/learners in my teaching session with same curiosity and exactly same question. I say the below to them with some of real world examples. As this is online now, i will try to make the explanation generic as possible. 

Businesses goal is to provide products or services and make profits. Each business consist of transactions(give or take) that are recorded in systems. In modern times is digitally recorded. Ancient days in was recorded in ledger and later tallied end of month say.

Process is a step to do an action.   Hence to achieve a transaction, following a certain steps are required. Simply a start, do some work and stop is also process with just 3 steps. So if you consider any business that takes the assets from one step to another to achieve a profit in exchange(give or take) for goods/services.

While doing so there are several parties or stakeholders' involved who has to be treated fairly. And this constitutes a system on its own for each party. Which means they have to interact to gain maximum from each of the transactions of course without discounting any fairness in business. This whole system of stakeholders shall use a single or interconnected system to automate the transactions in digital/software means. These systems should have to speak the single point of truth about the transaction at all point in times to showcase that it is fair.

In all these business steps the processes introduced by systems are generically termed as enterprises are planning their  resources.  As enterprises are the ones who think big, in scale and do business in all fairness. The leader in such enterprise software systems is SAP who is the provider of generic digital/software solutions' for each of the process. Hence the term business process is used always in the SAP ecosystem. Don't fret rather be logical in thinking how can I support a business grow in all fair means. 

To structure the learning start with understanding one domain(There are approximately 25 to 27 vertical domains) that SAP has software solutions. Say finance in a domain. Learn the process steps end to end to do a financial transaction. Keeping SAP or Technology aside just learn the finance as a domain, then all the process it has will come clear in your thought process. 

Apply that thought further to think how can you help each process step to get automated digitally via software solutions such that no one does repeated tasks that can be done easily by machines.  This will get simpler if you concentrate to practice on few learning paths/journey meant for students/freshers/newcomers in learning.sap.com   SAP Learning Hub

This is just the thought on top of my mind now, you shall always look up(google:-) for your experts in domain/business that are of your interest to explain it specially based on where ever you are stuck.

all the best

Regards

Lakshmi

entry.title: Insights on Columnar Data Storage
entry.content:
Introduction:

Instead of using conventional disk drives, an in-memory database is a kind of database management system that stores data directly in main memory (RAM) [3]. This design allows for real-time data processing, analytics, and transactional performance that greatly outperforms traditional systems because memory access is significantly faster than disk access. The one of primary characteristics of an in-memory database is,

Columnar Data Storage

Unlike traditional systems, which store data row by row, SAPHANA’s in-memory database stores data column by column. For instance:

Figure 1: How aColumn Store Table is Stored Physically

For example, the Orders table in the image above has four columns. OrderID, Amount, Shipping_country, and CustomerID.All the data in OrderID is stored first. Then the CustomerIDdata isstored, and so on. The Dictionary and Vector concept is used by HANA to improve CPU and memory utilization, speed up data access, and compress data.Dictionaryis a lookup table that contains all of the distinct values for every column is called a dictionary. HANA generates a distinct Dictionary table for every table column rather than storing raw values repeatedly in memory. Each column’s raw values are saved by HANA in the corresponding Dictionary table. Figure 2 shows how the dictionary table appears

 

Figure 2: Dictionary Tablefor Figure 1
For example, the Orders table’s Amount column contains the following values: 100, 200, 300, 100, and 150. In contrast, there are only four rows in the Amount column of the dictionary table above, with values of 100, 200, 300, and 150. The reason for this is that 100 is repeated.A vector is a condensed representation of every table column. It saves the Valueid from the matching dictionary table for every column value rather than repeatedly storing the raw values in memory

Figure 2: Vector Table for Figure 1. 

There are no raw values maintained in the picture above. Rather, each value’s Valueid is saved. As a result, it will minimize the amount of RAM needed to store the table

 

Comparative Advantages of Columnar Processing in Performance
Benefits Of Columnar Storage

Summary:

In-memory databases like SAP HANA store data in RAM for faster processing. They use columnar storage with dictionary and vector compression to reduce memory usage. This improves speed, efficiency, and overall performance for analytics and transactions.

entry.title: Re: No Ledger group and Name populating in Manage Journal...
entry.content:
Hello!

 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Manage Journal Entries app is primarily designed to display accounting documents at journal entry level

By default, it shows entries from the Leading Ledger (0L) even if no ledger group is selected.

When is not specified a ledger group, the system implicitly shows leading ledger postings, which is why can be seen many entries. 

The journal entry header does not explicitly store “ledger group” as a visible field in this app view.

 

Why results differ when filtering by Ledger Group (e.g. 0L):

  • When explicitly is selected Ledger Group = 0L, system applies strict filtering and returns only entries posted with that ledger group
  • When no Ledger Group is selected, system shows entries valid for leading ledger implicitly. Includes documents not explicitly tagged with ledger group

Many documents do not carry a specific ledger group assignment but still exist in 0L.

 

Alternative solution to verify ledger-specific postings can be used app “Display Line Items in General Ledger”.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content:
Good day @Rohit1105 

Thank you for your detailed explanation.

Your understanding is correct for this integration scenario. API_BUSINESS_PARTNER can create and update the BP, customer roles, addresses, contacts, and relationships, but it does not provide released entities to maintain SAP Collections Management attributes such as Collection Segment, etc. See KBA 3569467 for more details on the support for CRUD operations.

So, in simple terms:

  • You can create the BP and assign roles such as FLCU00, FLCU01 and UDM000.
  • The role UDM000 alone does not mean the full Collections Management profile and segment data is assigned.
  • The Collections Management attributes are managed by the Collections Management application logic, not by the standard BP API payload.
  • There is currently no standard release BP API entity for maintaining Collections Segment, Collection Group, etc.
  • The supported standard route is through Schedule Collections Management Jobs, not through direct inbound BP API update.

 

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/33653f80045b457bb178c01e02033534.html?locale=en-US

This means the supported design is a two-step process:

  1. First, you create or update the customer master data through the API
  2. Second, complete Collections Management assignment through the released Collections Management Job apps or manual Collections Management apps.

To your specific questions:

  • Regarding “Available APIs or extensibility options to maintain Collections Management data”. At present, there is no released standard API in API_BUSINESS_PARTNER to maintain these fields directly. You can do the relevant maintenance through Collections Management apps and jobs. For example, the Manage Business Partner-Collection Profile app is specifically used to create, edit, and manage collection specific data of a business partner.
  • Regarding if the Assignment of Collection Profiles to BP job be used: Yes, this is standard mass assignment approach. However, your concern about the fixed Created On date is valid. If the job templates require a fixed date and do not allow dynamic values such as “today” or today minus 1”, then it is not suitable as a fully dynamic daily automation without operational adjustment. See KBA 3586108 for more details.
  • On how the Collections Specialist should be handled. If you do not explicitly assign any specialist, the worklist generation can still automatically assign BP to collection specialists. This is described in KBA 3085530

This is important for your AR team using Process Receivables or collection worklists: if you don’t maintain an explicit specialist, the system can still distribute items during worklist generation, but not necessarily according to your external source system assignment logic.

I recommend you:

  1. Use API_BUSINESS_PARTNER only for the customer master data creation and role assignment. Then use Schedule Collections Management Jobs for the Collections Management data:
    1. Assign Collection Profile to BPs
    2. Change Collection Segment Data for BPs
    3. Replace Collection Specialists
    4. Assign Collection Specialists.

  1. Alternatively, you can use rule-based grouping instead of passing every attribute from Dynamics. If your collection setup can be derived from customer attributes already, define a rule-based operating model. For example, Company code, Country, Customer group, etc. Thereafter, use the Collections Management jobs to assign profiles and specialists based on these attributes where the job template supports the selection. This helps you avoid trying to push collection attributes through an API that does not expose them.

To summarise, the next step is to use a two-step design. Create and updated the BP from Microsoft Dynamics 365 through API_BUSINESS_PARTNER, then complete Collections Management assignment using Schedule Collections Management Jobs-Mass Changes and validate the result in Manage Business Partner-Collection Profile and Process Receivables.

You may also consider raising a product enhancement request with SAP for a released Collections Management API in Public Cloud.

Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Difference between movement type 657 and 657 E in customer returns (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,
When creating a return order with reference to a free-of-charge (FOC) order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the goods receipt (GR) is sometimes posted with movement type 657, and in other cases with 657 E (orders on hand).


In the same system, two return orders were created with reference to the same FOC order using the app Manage Customer Returns – Version 2.
 • For the first return order, the GR was posted with movement type 657 (no special stock). 
 • For the second return order, the GR was posted with movement type 657 E. 

Both return orders appear identical in terms of:
 • Item category 
 • Schedule line category 
 • Follow-up activity 

What causes the difference between movement type 657 and 657 E in this scenario?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Difference between movement type 657 and 657 E in cus...
entry.content:
Hi,

This behavior is standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and is related to Advanced Returns Management (ARM) logic.

The difference between movement type 657 and 657 E is not due to configuration inconsistencies, but is driven by the requirement type determination during the returns process.

Key difference

  • 657 (no E)
    → Stock is posted as normal returns stock
    → No sales order reference is assigned in the material document
  • 657 E
    → Stock is posted as special stock E (orders on hand / non-valuated returns stock)
    → The material document contains a sales order reference

 

Root cause

The system determines whether to use special stock E based on the requirement type:

  • If requirement type = MSR
    → Stock is posted to special stock E
    → Result: 657 E
  • If requirement type is blank
    → Stock is posted to normal stock
    → Result: 657

 

What influences the requirement type

The requirement type is determined automatically and depends on factors such as:

  • Refund control (e.g. “decide later” vs immediate credit)
  • Follow-up activity
  • Ownership / valuation logic in the returns process

For example:

  • Scenarios where ownership remains temporarily with the customer → MSR → special stock E
  • Scenarios where ownership is transferred immediately → no MSR → normal stock

 

Conclusion

Both postings (657 and 657 E) are correct and expected.

The difference reflects how the system manages stock ownership and valuation during the returns process, not a configuration issue.

entry.title: 1470000341 Fully allocate itemCode to bin locations in warehouse - InventoryGenEntry
entry.content:
Hy Experts,

I am trying to do a storno for issueForProduction by creating an inventoryGenEntry, the original document doesn't have BinAllocations, but it always shows the same error:

Item: C90501210, Qty: 0.62, Bin: 370
Item: C90501210, Qty: 0.62
Sztornó OIGN Error: 1470000341 - Fully allocate item "C90501210" to bin locations in warehouse "30"
Sztornó OIGN Error: -10

Here is my code:

if (process.Cancelled == true)

{

recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGN WHERE Comments like '%Sztornó-{process.SearchKey}%'");
if (recordSet.EoF)

{

recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGE WHERE Comments like '%{process.SearchKey}%'");
if (!recordSet.EoF)
{

string docEntryInventoryExit = recordSet.Fields.Item("DocEntry").Value.ToString();

SAPbobsCOM.Documents issueForProduction = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenExit);

int docEntryInventoryExitInt = int.Parse(docEntryInventoryExit);
bool foundInventoryExit = issueForProduction.GetByKey(docEntryInventoryExitInt);
Console.WriteLine($"DocEntry: {docEntryInventoryExitInt}, Found: {foundInventoryExit}");


SAPbobsCOM.Documents inventoryGenEntry = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry);

for (int i = 0; i < process.jobReportItems.Count; i++)
{

JobReportItemDTO items = process.jobReportItems[i];
if (items.Input == false)

continue;

SAPbobsCOM.Items item = (SAPbobsCOM.Items)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oItems);
if (item.GetByKey(items.Material))
{

DateTime StartDate;
if (DateTime.TryParse(process.StartTime, out StartDate))
{

inventoryGenEntry.DocDate = StartDate;

}
inventoryGenEntry.Comments = "dSuite azonosító: Sztornó-" + process.SearchKey + "\n" + process.Remarks;
inventoryGenEntry.DocObjectCode = SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry;
//inventoryGenEntry.DocumentsOwner = 44;
inventoryGenEntry.Reference2 = process.Employee;

int componentLine = -1;
string warehouse = "";
for (int j = 0; j < issueForProduction.Lines.Count; j++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.SetCurrentLine(j);

if (issueForProduction.Lines.ItemCode != items.Material)

continue;

double targetQty = Math.Abs(items.Quantity); // pl 0.62
double remainingQty = targetQty;
SAPbobsCOM.Recordset rsBin = (SAPbobsCOM.Recordset)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);

rsBin.DoQuery($@"
SELECT

OBTL.BinAbs,
OBTL.Quantity AS BinQty

FROM

OIGE

INNER JOIN IGE1 ON IGE1.DocEntry = OIGE.DocEntry

INNER JOIN OILM ON OILM.DocEntry = IGE1.DocEntry

AND OILM.DocLineNum = IGE1.LineNum

AND OILM.TransType = 60

INNER JOIN OBTL ON OBTL.MessageID = OILM.MessageID

INNER JOIN OBIN ON OBIN.AbsEntry = OBTL.BinAbs

WHERE

OIGE.DocEntry = {docEntryInventoryExitInt}

AND IGE1.ItemCode = '{items.Material}'
AND IGE1.LineNum = {issueForProduction.Lines.LineNum}
AND OBTL.BinAbs IS NOT NULL ");

if (rsBin.RecordCount > 0)
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType = (int)SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenExit;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = docEntryInventoryExitInt;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseLine = issueForProduction.Lines.LineNum;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.WarehouseCode = issueForProduction.Lines.WarehouseCode;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = targetQty;

if (issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count > 0)
{

double batchRemaining = targetQty;

for (int b = 0; b < issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count; b++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.SetCurrentLine(b);

double originalBatchQty = issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity;

if (batchRemaining <= 0)
break;

double qtyToUse = Math.Min(originalBatchQty, batchRemaining);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber =
issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity = qtyToUse;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.Add();

batchRemaining -= qtyToUse;

}

}

if (issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count > 0)
{

for (int s = 0; s < issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count; s++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.SetCurrentLine(s);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.SerialNumbers.InternalSerialNumber =
issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.InternalSerialNumber;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.SerialNumbers.Add();

}

}

rsBin.MoveFirst();

while (!rsBin.EoF)
{

int binEntry = (int)rsBin.Fields.Item("BinAbs").Value;
double qtyToUse = Convert.ToDouble(rsBin.Fields.Item("BinQty").Value);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = binEntry;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = qtyToUse;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber = 0;
if (issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count > 0 || issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count > 0)
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.SerialAndBatchNumbersBaseLine = 0;

}
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Add();
Console.WriteLine($"Item: {items.Material}, Qty: {qtyToUse}, Bin: {binEntry}");
rsBin.MoveNext();

}

}
else
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.ItemCode = items.Material;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.WarehouseCode = item.DefaultWarehouse;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = targetQty;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.AccountCode = "96930000";
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.CostingCode = "542GYÁRT";

int binEntry = 0;
SAPbobsCOM.Warehouses Defaultwarehouse = (SAPbobsCOM.Warehouses)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oWarehouses);
if (Defaultwarehouse.GetByKey(item.DefaultWarehouse))
{

binEntry = Defaultwarehouse.DefaultBin;
Console.WriteLine($"Default bin: {binEntry}");

}

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = binEntry;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = targetQty;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Add();

}
Console.WriteLine($"Item: {items.Material}, Qty: {targetQty}");
//Console.WriteLine($"{ inventoryGenEntry.GetAsXML()} ");
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Add();

break; 

}

}
else
{

Console.WriteLine("Nem található cikk a jelentéshez");

}

}


int result = inventoryGenEntry.Add();
if (result != 0)
{

Console.WriteLine($"Sztornó OIGN Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
Console.WriteLine($"Sztornó OIGN Error: {company.GetLastErrorCode()}");

}
else
{

Console.WriteLine("Alapanyag visszavételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");

}

}

}

}

Tried the BinActionType too but then the IDE sent me this:
'DocumentLinesBinAllocations' does not contain a definition for 'BinActionType' and no accessible extension method 'BinActionType' accepting a first argument of type 'DocumentLinesBinAllocations' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

entry.title: Updating Location Address Fails for Moved Business Partners in /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

there seems to be an issue with the program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR. The purpose of this program is to write the current address of the business partner to the corresponding location. This should update and correct the address in the location. However, this does not work if the business partner has moved. In that case, there are two standard addresses, but with different validity dates. If the program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR is run for a business partner who has moved, the following error message appears:

"Business partner 0123456789 has several standard addresses. Update not possible"

As a result, it is not possible to correct and keep the location address up to date for this business partner. The program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR only checks the existence of a standard address and fails to also look at the validity dates to determine the right address.

Are there any alternatives for updating the address in the location?
